cards.front: What are the two main goals of the LearnCPP tutorials?
cards.back:
• Teach C++ programming from scratch
  • Provide a comprehensive reference for programmers

cards.text: The LearnCPP tutorials are designed for {{c1::complete beginners}} and assume {{c2::no prior programming experience}}.

cards.front: Why should you read the tutorials sequentially rather than jumping to specific topics?
cards.back: Each lesson builds on concepts from previous lessons. Skipping lessons may result in missing important foundational knowledge needed to understand later topics.

cards.text: To learn programming effectively, you should {{c1::type in and compile}} the examples yourself rather than just {{c2::copying and pasting}} them.

cards.front: What should you do when you encounter a quiz question you cannot answer?
cards.back: Review the lesson material before checking the answer. The quizzes test your understanding of the material just covered.

cards.concept: The importance of experimentation in learning C++
cards.explanation: Experimenting with code examples helps you learn how C++ works in practice. Try modifying examples, breaking them intentionally, and predicting what will happen before running the code.
examples: Change variable values and observe output
Remove semicolons to see compiler errors
Modify code examples to solve different problems

cards.front: What makes the LearnCPP tutorials different from a traditional textbook?
cards.back: They are free, online, regularly updated with modern C++ practices, and designed to be more accessible and practical than traditional textbooks.

cards.text: These tutorials cover C++ through {{c1::C++20}}, with some coverage of {{c2::C++23}} features.

cards.front: Why is it important to do the practice problems and exercises?
cards.back: Practice problems reinforce learning and help identify gaps in understanding. They provide hands-on experience applying concepts from the lessons.

cards.front: What should you do if you get stuck on a problem or concept?
cards.back: Try to solve it yourself first, re-read the relevant lesson, then ask for help in the comments section or programming communities if still stuck.
